verses.text: Better the poor who walks in integrity than the perverse fool; without knowledge the soul is not good, and haste in foot misses the way; 'the foolishness of man perverteth his way: and his heart fretteth against the LORD.' Integrity beats riches. Men ruin themselves, then blame God.

Better the poor who walks in integrity than the perverse fool; without knowledge the soul is not good, and haste in foot misses the way; 'the foolishness of man perverteth his way: and his heart fretteth against the LORD.' Integrity beats riches. Men ruin themselves, then blame God.

Better the poor who walks in integrity than the perverse fool; without knowledge the soul is not good, and haste in foot misses the way; 'the foolishness of man perverteth his way: and his heart fretteth against the LORD.' Integrity beats riches. Men ruin themselves, then blame God.

Wealth makes many friends, but the poor is forsaken even by his neighbour; a false witness shall not go unpunished; many court the prince and the giver of gifts. Fair-weather friends abound. But God will not leave deceit unpunished.

Wealth makes many friends, but the poor is forsaken even by his neighbour; a false witness shall not go unpunished; many court the prince and the giver of gifts. Fair-weather friends abound. But God will not leave deceit unpunished.

Wealth makes many friends, but the poor is forsaken even by his neighbour; a false witness shall not go unpunished; many court the prince and the giver of gifts. Fair-weather friends abound. But God will not leave deceit unpunished.

The poor man's brothers hate him, and his friends keep distant; 'he that getteth wisdom loveth his own soul'; 'a false witness shall not be unpunished, and he that speaketh lies shall perish.' To gain wisdom is to love oneself. Lying brings sure ruin.

Luxury suits not a fool, nor a servant to rule princes; 'the discretion of a man deferreth his anger; and it is his glory to pass over a transgression'; the king's wrath is as a lion's roar, his favour as dew. To overlook a wrong is glory. Patience is the mark of a wise man.

Luxury suits not a fool, nor a servant to rule princes; 'the discretion of a man deferreth his anger; and it is his glory to pass over a transgression'; the king's wrath is as a lion's roar, his favour as dew. To overlook a wrong is glory. Patience is the mark of a wise man.

A foolish son and a brawling wife are a continual dripping; 'house and riches are the inheritance of fathers: and a prudent wife is from the LORD'; slothfulness casts into deep sleep, and the idle shall suffer hunger. A good wife is God's gift. Laziness brings only want.

A foolish son and a brawling wife are a continual dripping; 'house and riches are the inheritance of fathers: and a prudent wife is from the LORD'; slothfulness casts into deep sleep, and the idle shall suffer hunger. A good wife is God's gift. Laziness brings only want.

A foolish son and a brawling wife are a continual dripping; 'house and riches are the inheritance of fathers: and a prudent wife is from the LORD'; slothfulness casts into deep sleep, and the idle shall suffer hunger. A good wife is God's gift. Laziness brings only want.

He that keeps the commandment keeps his soul; 'he that hath pity upon the poor lendeth unto the LORD,' who will repay him; chasten thy son while there is hope. Kindness to the poor is a loan to God. Discipline a child in love, before it is too late.

A man of great wrath shall suffer punishment; hear counsel and receive instruction, that thou mayest be wise; 'there are many devices in a man's heart; nevertheless the counsel of the LORD, that shall stand.' Heed wise counsel. Whatever men plan, God's purpose alone prevails.

The desire of a man is his kindness, and a poor man is better than a liar; 'the fear of the LORD tendeth to life,' and he that has it shall abide satisfied; the slothful will not even bring his hand to his mouth. Reverence brings life. Kindness and honesty outweigh riches.

Smite a scorner, and the simple beware; reprove the wise, and he gains knowledge; he that wastes his father is a shameful son; cease to hear instruction and you err from knowledge; judgments are prepared for scorners. The wise learn from a word. But scorners must be punished, and the foolish reap shame.
